title: The Wall of Pawns
setup: Black's pawn shield on **f7**, **g7**, **h7** is intact — but with no escape window, that's a death sentence.
hint: Slide the rook up the open file — the {{f7}}-{{g7}}-{{h7}} pawns do all the work.
explanation:
## The Idea

The **back-rank mate** in its purest form: an open file, an empty back rank, and a Black pawn shield with zero escape squares.

Play {{Rd8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{f8}} and {{h8}} — covered by the rook on d8 (8th rank)
- {{f7}}, {{g7}}, {{h7}} — all blocked by Black's own pawns
- Nothing on the d8-g8 line can interpose
- The king cannot capture: d8 is far from g8

> **Takeaway:** This is the position every chess teacher uses on day one. The fix is simple — push *one* of the f, g, or h pawns one square forward to make an escape window before the rook arrives.
